objective:
[Scalp de continuation / micro-retournement] sur [crypto liquide] [5m ou 15m].
Indicateurs : [EMA 9/21/50] + [RSI 14] + [Bandes de Bollinger 20,2].
Entrées : [pullback vers EMA 21 avec RSI repartant dans le sens de la micro-tendance et rejet d’une bande] / [excès sur bande externe avec RSI en surachat/survente et signal de retournement court terme].
Sorties : [take-profit sur extension vers bande opposée ou prochain niveau de structure] / [sortie anticipée si clôture franche contre la position ou divergence RSI].
Risk management : [stop-loss sous dernier plus bas/haut local ou 1× ATR] / [ratio minimum 1,5] / [risque fixe 1–2 % du capital par trade].

iterations.hypothesis: This strategy combines Bollinger Bands for volatility assessment, RSI for overbought/oversold conditions, and EMA for trend confirmation. The idea is to enter when price pulls back to the EMA with RSI indicating strength in the direction of the trend and rejects a Bollinger Band, or when it breaks out of an external Bollinger Band with RSI showing extreme levels and a short-term reversal signal.

The previous version had too many signals due to its simplistic entry conditions. By adding more nuanced filters such as stricter RSI thresholds and improved band rejection logic, the strategy should become less overtraded while maintaining profitability.

iterations.hypothesis: This strategy combines the Bollinger Bands to identify overextended price levels and potential reversals, RSI to gauge momentum strength and potential trend changes, and EMA to filter signals based on trend direction. The combination of these indicators should help in capturing scalp trades at micro-trend continuations or reversals with improved risk-adjusted returns.

iterations.hypothesis: This strategy combines EMA 21 and RSI to capture pullbacks in the direction of the trend. The EMA acts as a support/resistance level, while RSI ensures the asset isn't overbought or oversold, reducing false signals.
